title: Eureiandra fasciculata
intro: Eureiandra fasciculata, commonly known as the "Malaysian spikenard," is a plant species belonging to the family Cucurbitaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain, due to its potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Eureiandra fasciculata include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Eureiandra fasciculata is often used to make tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat conditions such as arthritis and muscle spasms.
